result.stdout:
Description:
  This .NET tool allows you to create a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for a given directory. It also allows you to add the SBOM to a package.

Usage:
  Skyline.DataMiner.CICD.Tools.Sbom [command] [options]

Options:
  --minimum-log-level <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Indicates what the minimum log level should be. Default is Information
  --version                                                            Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                                                       Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add               Adds the specified SBOM to the DataMiner package.
  generate          Generates a SBOM file for the provided directory.
  generate-and-add  Generates a SBOM file for the provided directory and adds it to the provided package.

commands.payload:
Description:
  This .NET tool allows you to create a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for a given directory. It also allows you to add the SBOM to a package.

Usage:
  Skyline.DataMiner.CICD.Tools.Sbom [command] [options]

Options:
  --minimum-log-level <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Indicates what the minimum log level should be. Default is Information
  --version                                                            Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                                                       Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add               Adds the specified SBOM to the DataMiner package.
  generate          Generates a SBOM file for the provided directory.
  generate-and-add  Generates a SBOM file for the provided directory and adds it to the provided package.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Adds the specified SBOM to the DataMiner package.

Usage:
  Skyline.DataMiner.CICD.Tools.Sbom add [options]

Options:
  -s, --sbom-file <sbom-file> (REQUIRED)                               The SBOM file path.
  -p, --package-file <package-file> (REQUIRED)                         The package file path to add the SBOM file to.
  -o, --output <output>                                                The output directory to place the package with the SBOM file included.
  --minimum-log-level <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Indicates what the minimum log level should be. Default is Information
  -?, -h, --help                                                       Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Adds the specified SBOM to the DataMiner package.

Usage:
  Skyline.DataMiner.CICD.Tools.Sbom add [options]

Options:
  -s, --sbom-file <sbom-file> (REQUIRED)                               The SBOM file path.
  -p, --package-file <package-file> (REQUIRED)                         The package file path to add the SBOM file to.
  -o, --output <output>                                                The output directory to place the package with the SBOM file included.
  --minimum-log-level <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Indicates what the minimum log level should be. Default is Information
  -?, -h, --help                                                       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generates a SBOM file for the provided directory.

Usage:
  Skyline.DataMiner.CICD.Tools.Sbom generate [options]

Options:
  -s, --solution-path <solution-path> (REQUIRED)                       The directory containing the solution or the solution file itself
  -pn, --package-name <package-name>                                   The name of the package the SBOM represents. Will default to the solution directory name.
  -pv, --package-version <package-version> (REQUIRED)                  The version of the package the SBOM represents.
  -ps, --package-supplier <package-supplier> (REQUIRED)                The supplier of the package the SBOM represents.
  -o, --output <output> (REQUIRED)                                     The directory where the SBOM file will be placed.
  --minimum-log-level <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Indicates what the minimum log level should be. Default is Information
  -?, -h, --help                                                       Show help and usage information
